column22:
V1: "Blau needs a mnemonic device to better understand the topic of microcontrollers. (1) He imagines how a small blue chip in a tiny computer controls all commands. (2) This microcontroller stores programs in memory and processes inputs via sensors. (3) With its fast clock frequency, it ensures that everything runs smoothly. (4) This helps Blue remember the important functions of the microcontroller in a vivid way. (5)"
V2: Imagine a small blue chip as a mini-computer that turns lights on and off at its pins—sometimes high, sometimes low. This is how it controls devices and runs programs written in C++
V3: "Mikro the janitor (the microcontroller) plugs the connector into the positive and negative terminals, flips the switches on his pins up (HIGH) or down (LOW), and follows his schedule exactly—the program in C++ that tells him what to do and when. (suggestion of the student)
V4: "Imagine that Mikro, the little blue chip, is a wizard who uses his magic wands (the pins) to turn the light on (HIGH) and off (LOW) so that the machine does exactly what his spell book (program in C++) tells it to do.

column22:
Turn 13: 
"A microcontroller, small and fine,  
is like a conductor in an orchestra.  
It switches pins to high and low,  
controls voltage, makes it so.
It considers plus and minus,  
runs programs, pays attention.  
Its actions are written in C++,  
so it never lets devices rest.
It controls everything you like,  
a small computer, day after day.
Turn 13 in german:
„Ein Mikrocontroller, klein und fein,  
ist wie ein Dirigent im Verein.  
Er schaltet Pins auf high und low,  
steuert Spannung, macht sie so.
Mit Plus und Minus wird er bedacht,  
führt Programme, gibt auf acht.  
In C++ schreibt man sein Tun,  
so lässt er Geräte niemals ruh'n.
Er steuert alles, was man mag,  
ein kleiner Computer, Tag für Tag.“
